archetype: Real-Time Cryogenic Freezer Row Thermal Stratification & Inert Gas Quench Predictor for Industrial Liquid Nitrogen Storage
domain: Industrial Cryogenic Infrastructure & Cold Chain Logistics
description: A deterministic control system that models multi-phase fluid dynamics within massive liquid nitrogen storage vessels to predict thermal layer collapse and prevent catastrophic pressure spikes during rapid draw-off. It is used by facility engineers in semiconductor manufacturing and biobanking to orchestrate safety valves and purge cycles, requiring millisecond-latency fusion of ultrasonic level sensors, distributed fiber-optic thermometry (DTS), and pressure transducers against non-linear thermodynamic equations.
rules: MUST ingest data via Modbus TCP and IEC 61850 standards from legacy industrial PLCs while maintaining sub-100ms end-to-end latency for emergency shutdown triggers.
MUST comply with ASME BPVC Section VIII Division 1 standards for pressure vessel safety margins and OSHA 1910.119 Process Safety Management requirements.
MUST implement ISO 11014 safety data sheet protocols to dynamically adjust alarm thresholds based on real-time ambient humidity and barometric pressure inputs.
MUST support edge-computed CFD approximations (Computational Fluid Dynamics) to predict stratification events before they occur, filtering sensor noise without cloud dependency.
MUST ensure data integrity via NIST-traceable calibration logs and immutable audit trails for every valve actuation and setpoint change.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use asynchronous event loops for safety-critical valve control, as garbage collection pauses or context switches could delay emergency isolation beyond the critical time window.

DO NOT rely on approximate floating-point arithmetic for volume calculations; use fixed-point or arbitrary-precision libraries to prevent cumulative rounding errors that could trigger false 'over-fill' alarms.
DO NOT design the UI for human-in-the-loop decision making during a thermal runaway event; the system must autonomously execute pre-certified mitigation sequences without human confirmation.
key_technical_challenges: Synchronizing disparate sensor sampling rates (10Hz DTS vs 1kHz pressure transducers) to create a coherent temporal view of fluid dynamics without losing phase alignment.
Solving the 'two-phase flow' simulation problem at the edge, where the software must approximate liquid-gas interface movement in a complex geometry with limited compute resources.
Managing the 'dead zone' hysteresis in cryogenic control valves to prevent chattering that causes metal fatigue and catastrophic mechanical failure.

recommended_stack.language: Rust — provides memory safety without garbage collection, essential for hard real-time control loops in safety-critical environments.
recommended_stack.framework: Tokio combined with a deterministic RTOS abstraction layer — ensures task scheduling predictability while handling high-throughput I/O.
recommended_stack.database_engine: TimescaleDB (PostgreSQL extension) — optimized for high-velocity time-series ingestion with continuous aggregates for real-time trend analysis.
recommended_stack.cache: ZeroMQ (embedded) — ultra-low latency message passing for inter-process communication between sensor collectors and the control logic.
recommended_stack.message_broker: Null (Direct shared memory or ZeroMQ) — eliminates the network hop latency of a central broker for critical safety signals.
special_services: Edge-CFD Solver Module for real-time stratification prediction
Hysteresis Compensation Engine for Valve Actuation Smoothing
